chief_complaint: Chest tightness with dyspnea in patient with known heart failure
clinical_reasoning: 68-year-old male with acute chest tightness and breathing difficulty. SpO2 borderline at 94%. Known heart failure and atrial fibrillation on warfarin. Mild tachycardia (HR 92) and hypertension (145/85). The combination of cardiac history with acute symptoms and borderline oxygenation warrants ORANGE — very urgent assessment within 20 minutes.
